ELON MUSK'S STARLINK IS FACING A FRESH REGULATORY CHALLENGE IN INDIA AS AUTHORITIES RAISE CONCERNS OVER HOW THE SATELLITE INTERNET PROVIDER ROUTES USER DATA.

ACCORDING TO REPORTS, INDIAN OFFICIALS ARE SCRUTINIZING STARLINK'S USE OF INTER-SATELLITE LASER LINKS, WHICH CAN TRANSMIT DATA BETWEEN SATELLITES AND POTENTIALLY ROUTE INTERNET TRAFFIC THROUGH GATEWAYS LOCATED OUTSIDE THE COUNTRY. REGULATORS WANT DATA GENERATED IN INDIA TO PASS EXCLUSIVELY THROUGH DOMESTIC GATEWAYS TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH NATIONAL SECURITY AND LAWFUL INTERCEPTION REQUIREMENTS.

THE GOVERNMENT IS REPORTEDLY CONSIDERING RESTRICTIONS ON STARLINK'S LASER-LINKED SATELLITE NETWORK FOR INDIA-BASED TRAFFIC. IF IMPLEMENTED, THE COMPANY MAY BE REQUIRED TO DISABLE LASER-LINK ROUTING FOR INDIAN USERS AND INSTEAD DIRECT TRAFFIC THROUGH GROUND INFRASTRUCTURE LOCATED WITHIN THE COUNTRY. ...
